witnesses.description: A two-variable active-set QP witness for min (x-2)^2 + (y-1)^2 subject to x <= 1 and y >= 0.
A degenerate active-set QP witness where the unconstrained minimizer already lies on the active bound x <= 1, so the tight active constraint has zero multiplier.

checks.claim: The unconstrained minimizer of (x-2)^2 + (y-1)^2 is (2,1), but it violates x <= 1 by one unit.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes the objective, gradient, and active-bound violation exactly.

checks.claim: Fixing the active face x=1 and solving the free coordinate gives candidate (1,1) with objective value 1.
checks.notes: The validator checks the active equality, free-coordinate gradient, and exact objective value.

checks.claim: At (1,1), active multiplier 2 and inactive multiplier 0 satisfy KKT stationarity and complementary slackness.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes gradient + lambda*a + mu*b and both multiplier-slack products.

checks.claim: The lower-bound constraint y >= 0 is inactive at (1,1), with slack 1 and zero multiplier.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes the inactive constraint value, slack, and complementarity product.

checks.claim: At the active-set candidate (1,1), the lower-bound constraint y >= 0 is tight.

data.certificate: The resource regression parses the source SMT-LIB artifact, emits UnsatFarkas evidence, and independently checks the certificate.

checks.notes: Exact replay computes inactive slack = 0 - (-1) = 1 for the lower-bound constraint, so the malformed tight/nonpositive-slack claim is rejected by checked Farkas evidence.

checks.claim: The feasible point (1,0) solves the same active-face subproblem.

data.certificate: The resource regression parses the source SMT-LIB artifact, emits UnsatFarkas evidence, and independently checks the certificate.

checks.notes: Exact replay computes free-coordinate stationarity error 2 for (1,0), so the malformed nonpositive-error claim is rejected by checked Farkas evidence.

checks.claim: For f(x,y)=(x-1)^2+y^2 with x <= 1, the minimizer (1,0) is feasible and tight on the active bound with zero multiplier.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes the zero gradient, tight active constraint, zero multiplier, and zero stationarity residual over exact rationals.

checks.claim: The same degenerate active-bound optimum accepts a positive active multiplier lambda=1 while preserving stationarity.

data.certificate: The resource regression parses the source SMT-LIB artifact, emits UnsatFarkas evidence, and independently checks the certificate.

checks.notes: Exact replay computes zero gradient at a tight bound; any positive multiplier along normal (1,0) leaves stationarity error 1, so the malformed row is rejected by checked Farkas evidence.

checks.claim: General active-set finite termination, degeneracy handling, and convergence theorems require future Lean reconstruction.

data.target_theorem_shape: Finite termination and correctness of active-set methods for convex quadratic programs under nondegeneracy assumptions, plus degeneracy and cycling safeguards
data.future_checker: Lean module with no sorryAx dependencies plus exact rational certificate bridges for finite active-set QP witnesses

checks.notes: The finite rows check one rational active-face QP instance; active-set algorithm theory remains proof-assistant work.
